title: Country Shoe Bench
summary: A compact 36"-wide standalone shoe bench with a single divider splitting two open shelves, built from 1x12 and 1x3 — a small freestanding piece, unlike this catalog's built-in shoe bench.
description:
This bench is sized to stand on its own, not to be fitted between two walls — two 1x12 shelves span a single center divider, all closed in by two 1x12 side panels, giving a compact two-bay shoe shelf under a plain 1x12 top. At 36" wide, it's a genuinely small entryway piece rather than a long built-in.

A 1x3 trim strip caps the top on both the front and back edges, giving the flat top a finished look without a full apron. Use a jigsaw to clean up any cuts on the side panels if a decorative edge or foot detail is wanted, though the plan works fine as plain rectangles too.

Fill nail holes with wood filler before finishing, since brad nails are the primary fastener throughout this build and the holes show on painted surfaces more than stained ones.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the shelf and divider assembly
steps.body: Attach the two shelves to the center divider and the two side panels.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top board to the assembled sides and shelves.

steps.title: Add the top trim
steps.body: Miter and nail the top trim to the front and back edges of the top.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Fill nail holes with wood filler, sand, and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly
